hero.title: 6 Days, Amritsar & Dharamsala.
hero.titleAccent: The Golden Temple and the Home of the Dalai Lama.
hero.intro: Two very different souls of North India. Amritsar's Golden Temple and Punjabi warmth, then up into the hills to Dharamshala and McLeod Ganj, the home of the Dalai Lama and Tibetan culture in exile.
hero.heroImageAlt: Dharamshala McLeod Ganj Tibetan monastery Himalayas

overview.heading: 6 Days Amritsar + Dharamsala: The Full Picture
body: This 6 day journey pairs the spiritual heart of Sikhism with the centre of Tibetan Buddhism in exile. Amritsar brings the Golden Temple, the Wagah border and Punjabi food, while Dharamshala and McLeod Ganj, set in the Himalayan foothills, are home to the Dalai Lama, Tibetan monasteries and a serene mountain atmosphere.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip moves from the plains to the hills with your own vehicle and driver guide. The contrast between Amritsar's golden grandeur and Dharamshala's peaceful Tibetan culture makes for a rich, varied North India experience.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Golden Temple, Amritsar
items.body: Harmandir Sahib at dawn and after dark, the langar, and the moving spiritual atmosphere of Sikhism's holiest shrine.

items.title: McLeod Ganj & the Dalai Lama
items.body: The Tsuglagkhang Complex, home of the Dalai Lama, the Tibetan monasteries, prayer flags and the relaxed mountain town of McLeod Ganj.

items.title: Himalayan Foothills
items.body: Pine forests, mountain views, the Bhagsu waterfall and the serene Norbulingka Institute of Tibetan arts in the hills around Dharamshala.

days.title: Arrive Amritsar
days.description: Met at Amritsar airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at the illuminated Golden Temple.

days.title: Amritsar & Wagah Border
days.description: Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh and the Wagah border ceremony.

days.title: Amritsar to Dharamshala
days.description: Scenic drive up into the Himalayan foothills.

days.title: McLeod Ganj Sightseeing
days.description: The Dalai Lama's complex, monasteries and Tibetan culture.

days.title: Dharamshala Leisure
days.description: More of the hills, with the cricket stadium, monasteries and viewpoints.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Descend for your onward flight from Dharamshala or Amritsar.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Plains and Peaks, One Smooth Journey
whyBook.body: Linking Amritsar and Dharamshala means coordinating the plains and the hills with a mountain drive. We handle the route and the timing so the contrast becomes the journey's strength. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Amritsar & Dharamsala Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 6 day package pairs two of North India's most distinctive destinations: Amritsar, with its Golden Temple, and Dharamshala, the home of the Dalai Lama and Tibetan culture in exile.

A private vehicle and driver guide link the plains and the hills, with the scenic drive up to the foothills part of the experience.

The contrast between Sikh grandeur and Tibetan serenity makes this one of the most rewarding short tours in the north.

sections.heading: Dharamshala and McLeod Ganj
sections.body:
Dharamshala and the upper town of McLeod Ganj have been the seat of the Tibetan government in exile and the home of the Dalai Lama since 1959. The Tsuglagkhang Complex, the monasteries and the prayer flags give the town a deeply peaceful, spiritual atmosphere.

Set in the Himalayan foothills with pine forests and mountain views, it is a serene counterpoint to the energy of Amritsar.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Amritsar or Dharamshala (Gaggal) Airport (ATQ / DHM). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Will I see the Dalai Lama?
items.answer: The Dalai Lama occasionally gives public teachings at the Tsuglagkhang Complex in McLeod Ganj, but his schedule varies and is not guaranteed. The complex, monasteries and Tibetan culture are wonderful to experience regardless. We can advise if any teachings coincide with your dates.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: March to June and September to November offer the most pleasant weather across both the plains and the hills. Winter brings cold and possible snow to Dharamshala. The monsoon (July to August) is lush but can affect hill roads.

items.question: How far is Dharamshala from Amritsar?
items.answer: Dharamshala is around 5 to 6 hours by road from Amritsar, a scenic drive up into the Himalayan foothills. The private vehicle makes the journey comfortable, with stops along the way.
